summary: Build 209 is a LOCAL-ONLY SMS workflow/message-copy analysis and revision packet for the next controlled pilot send. The real-customer pilot SMS (Jason-operated, Build 205) proved live delivery TECHNICALLY, but the message content was generic delivery-test copy only ('RoofLeadHQ controlled live test: one-message SMS to your own consenting test identity (Test Roofing). No action needed.') that did NOT validate RoofLeadHQ value. This build analyzes that gap and drafts revised roofer-facing SMS copy variants for review, records the homeowner-facing copy boundary (NOT approved now), records the next decision packet (COPY_REVISED_REVIEW_REQUIRED, recommended_next_option=review_copy_before_any_live_send), and includes an UNSIGNED next-send approval template. It performs NO live action: no SMS, no Twilio, no network/external call, no retry, no roofer/homeowner contact, and reads/records no secret values, SIDs, tokens, credential values, phone numbers, or production data. It does NOT approve any send, expansion, homeowner contact, live automation, or unrestricted launch. Launch remains pilot-gated, NOT unrestricted. Safety posture preserved: demo_ready_with_live_automation_disabled.

message_copy_analysis_evidence.current_message_text_for_analysis: RoofLeadHQ controlled live test: one-message SMS to your own consenting test identity (Test Roofing). No action needed.
message_copy_analysis_evidence.analysis_notes: The text above proved the live SMS delivery path works end-to-end (one message, Jason-operated, no retry), but it is a pure delivery probe: it describes the test itself, asks for no action, and demonstrates none of RoofLeadHQ's value (fast lead response, inspection-request handling, missed-lead prevention, faster follow-up). The next controlled send should use workflow-appropriate roofer-facing copy that reflects an actual RoofLeadHQ moment, while keeping all messaging honest (no guarantees, no booked-jobs claims, no estimate/quote/invoice/payment/deposit claims) and SMS-only.

variants.label: new_lead_fast_response_alert
variants.text: RoofLeadHQ: New roofing lead just came in. A quick first reply now helps you reach them while they're still looking. Reply STOP to opt out.

variants.label: missed_lead_prevention_nudge
variants.text: RoofLeadHQ: A lead from earlier still has no first reply. A short follow-up now helps you avoid losing them to a faster response elsewhere. Reply STOP to opt out.

variants.label: inspection_request_flag
variants.text: RoofLeadHQ: A homeowner asked about a roof inspection. Want us to flag it for follow-up? Reply with your next step. Reply STOP to opt out.

variants.label: faster_follow_up_daily_nudge
variants.text: RoofLeadHQ: A few new leads are still waiting on a first reply today. Faster follow-up keeps more of them engaged. Reply STOP to opt out.

variants.label: open_follow_up_recap
variants.text: RoofLeadHQ recap: leads received and follow-ups still open today. Reply RECAP for the list. Reply STOP to opt out.

draft_principles_only: identify the roofing business clearly by name in the first line
reference the homeowner's own prior inquiry or inspection request (never cold contact)
obtain and record per-homeowner consent BEFORE any homeowner SMS
state plainly why they are being contacted and what to do next
include clear opt-out language (Reply STOP to opt out)
honor quiet hours in the roofer's local timezone
no guarantees, no pressure, no booked-jobs or estimate/quote/invoice/payment/deposit claims

placeholder_examples_not_approved.label: homeowner_inspection_followup_draft
placeholder_examples_not_approved.text: [DRAFT - NOT APPROVED] Hi, this is [Roofer Name] following up on your roof inspection request. Reply YES to set a time, or STOP to opt out.

homeowner_facing_copy_boundary.placeholder_note: The homeowner example above is a DRAFT for future review only. It is NOT approved, NOT consented, and NOT to be sent. Any homeowner-facing message requires separate, explicit consent capture and a separate signed approval.

unsigned_next_send_approval_template.purpose: UNSIGNED template for a future controlled pilot send AFTER Jason reviews the revised copy. This file authorizes NOTHING. It documents the shape of the per-attempt signed approval Jason would complete OUTSIDE this build before any live send. It contains no secret values, no phone numbers, no SIDs, no tokens, and no production data.
